tasks.description: SAML ACS binds identity to the document, not the signed element → signature wrapping (XSW). auth/src/saml.rs:177-191 extracts NameID/Issuer/Audience from the whole SAMLResponse via naive first-match string parsing (extract_xml_element/extract_xml_attr, saml.rs:529-559); saml_sig::verify (saml.rs:277) returns only signed_id and does not constrain extraction to the signed subtree. An attacker holding any one validly-signed assertion for the tenant's IdP can wrap it and inject an attacker-controlled NameID/Issuer elsewhere in the XML; the signature still verifies but the injected identity is consumed → impersonation of arbitrary users within a SAML-enabled tenant (Critical if a signed assertion is readily obtainable). Compounded by the audience check being skipped when <Audience> is absent (saml.rs:257, if let Some(aud)). Expected delta: re-extract NameID/Issuer/Audience from WITHIN the element verified by signed_id (reject if they fall outside it); require an AudienceRestriction; add an XSW/wrapping regression test that must be rejected.
